methods.method_name: Extracellular Current Measurement using Vibrating Probe
methods.description: Describes the use of a newly developed vibrating probe system with a spherical, platinum-black electrode to measure extracellular voltage differences between two points and calculate current density around biological samples like pollen tubes. It details the principle of operation involving vibration, lock-in amplification, and calculation based on electric field and medium conductivity. It also mentions improvements made to eliminate artifactual signals.

steps.description: Vibrate the probe at about 200 cycles per second in a horizontal plane between two extracellular points 30 µm apart.
steps.searchable_summary: Vibrate probe between two extracellular points.
outputs: Sinusoidal voltage output corresponding to voltage difference

steps.description: Convert the steady voltage difference between the points into a sinusoidal output measurable with the aid of a lock-in amplifier tuned to the vibration frequency.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure sinusoidal output with lock-in amplifier.

steps.description: Calculate the electric field, which is approximately equal to the voltage difference divided by the distance (30 µm).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate electric field from voltage difference and distance.

steps.description: Calculate the current density in the direction of vibration by multiplying the electric field by the medium's conductivity.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate current density from electric field and medium conductivity.

methods.method_name: Preparation and Growth of Pollen Tubes on Membrane
methods.description: Describes the method for supporting and growing pollen tubes horizontally on a thin, ion-permeable cellulose membrane supported by a nickel screen. This setup prevents the pollen tubes from being moved by the fluid flow generated by the vibrating electrode, allowing for stable measurements.

steps.description: Support a thin (3 µm wet thickness), tough, and ion-permeable cellulose membrane with an appropriate nickel screen.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare membrane support system for pollen growth.

steps.description: Spread out dry pollen on a clean dry surface.
Blot excess fluid off the lower surface of the screen-supported membrane.
steps.searchable_summary: Blot excess fluid from membrane surface.

steps.description: Brush the dry and separated pollen grains onto this blotted surface.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply pollen to blotted membrane surface.

steps.description: Allow enough fluid to ooze through the membrane within a few minutes after deposition of a grain to swell and activate the grain.
steps.searchable_summary: Activate pollen grains on membrane using fluid.

steps.description: Grow pollen tubes under this membrane while the bulk of the medium, as well as the vibrating probe, is above it.

Ensure the tubes are kept horizontal as they grow and are not moved by the vibrating probe by maintaining contact with the membrane throughout almost all of their growth, aided by surface forces from fluid sleeves and apparent negative geotropism.
steps.searchable_summary: Maintain horizontal growth and stability of pollen tubes on membrane.
outputs: Stable, horizontally growing pollen tubes suitable for measurement
